architecture.summary: JEPA-Reasoner decouples latent-space reasoning from token generation. The Reasoner component (embedding layer + modified Transformer blocks with hybrid normalization) generates a complete reasoning chain autoregressively within a normalized latent space using scaled cosine similarity loss and EMA target encoder weights. A separate Talker module (Mono-Talker or Dual-Talker variants) then reconstructs tokens from the frozen reasoning trajectory. Information flows: input tokens → Reasoner latent chain (looped back within unit hypersphere) → Talker → output tokens. Token errors cannot backpropagate into reasoning because the Reasoner conditions only on latent states, not on sampled tokens.

biblio.title: JEPA-Reasoner: Decoupling Latent Reasoning from Token Generation

claims.statement: JEPA-Reasoner achieves 149.5% improvement in 8-shot GSM8K accuracy over a coupled Transformer baseline trained on identical data
JEPA-Reasoner 8-shot GSM8K accuracy is 51.9% versus Llama 3.2-1B (CoT) 44.4%
JEPA-Reasoner improves 5-shot to 8-shot GSM8K performance by 131.7% (from 39.4% to 51.9%), whereas Transformer baseline stagnates at 0.1% improvement
On CFG token-level error robustness test, JEPA-Reasoner maintains higher accuracy than Transformer baseline under 0–30% token corruption across 4 generation steps

claims.dataset: synthetic binary tree search (42M model, 384D latent)
claims.metric: exact match accuracy; plane distance ranking (percent)
claims.statement: Tree-search task (binary tree depth 4): JEPA-Reasoner + Mono-Talker achieves 99.87% exact match accuracy, with predicted latent vectors forming a continuous cloud within the space spanned by discrete vocabulary embeddings, and sibling-node planes exhibiting average top ranking of 1.72% distance to predicted vectors

JEPA-Reasoner generates mixed latent vectors representing multiple possible reasoning paths simultaneously; for 99.89% of cases, the latent vector of the node on the correct route contributes more than the sibling node in linear combinations

By decoupled architecture design, the sensitivity of future reasoning state with respect to current generated token satisfies ∂z_t'/∂x_t = 0, proving no mathematical pathway for token-space errors to propagate into latent reasoning trajectory

L2 normalization constrains all latent states to the unit hypersphere, bounding Euclidean distance between predicted and optimal reasoning states by the hypersphere diameter; scaled cosine distance loss implicitly minimizes angular divergence from optimal trajectory
